content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Pat Miranda 
Mayor 
City of Irwindale 
5050 North Irwindale Avenue 
Irwindale, CA 
91706 
Dear Mayor Miranda: 
December 5, 1985 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-85-248 
This is in response to your letter dated November 26, 1985 
in which you sought the Commission's advice regarding a 
potential conflict of interest under Section 1090 of the 
Government Code. 
Although the Commission is authorized to render advice 
regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act 
(Government Code sections 81000-91015), we have not been 
authorized to give advice regarding other conflict of interest 
laws such as Government Code section 1090. 
The commission 
realizes that it would be more convenient for public officials, 
such as yourself, to have all of their conflict of interest 
questions answered by one agency, and the Commission has 
directed its staff to explore possible alternatives to bring 
this about. 
However, at this time no concrete proposal has 
been formulated. 
Therefore, lacking any authority to render advice regarding 
section 1090, we must decline your request and instead 
recommend that you contact the Attorney General's Office for 
such advice. 
The Commission cannot request an opinion from the 
Attorney General on your behalf; however, your representatives 
in the Legislature may'do so. 


Pat Miranda 
December 5, 1985 
Page 2 
If we can ever be of assistance to you regarding your 
responsibilities under the Political Reform Act, please do not 
hesitate to contact us again. 
REL:JG:plh 
Sincerely, 
~J7 it.U-j,:i ;::} ,.L:/,-
~bert E. ~ 
7 
Counsel 
Legal Division 


CITY OF IR 
5050 NORTH IRWINDALE AVENUE . IRWINDALE, CALIFORNIA 91706 
(213) 962-3381 
November 26, 1985 
Fair Political Practices 
Commission 
Suite 800 
428 J Street 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Attention: 
Mr. Leydeigh 
Dear Mr. Leydeigh: 
My name is Pat Hiranda, and I am the Mayor of the City of 
Irwindale and Chairman of its Co~~unity Redevelopment Agency. 
N

C 2 
R 
3 C

Referring to the diagram above, I own Parcell; another council-
man owns Parcel 2; still another councilman owns Parcel 5. 
Parcels 1, 2, 3, 4 and 6 are zoned cOITmercial; Parcel 5 is 
zoned residential. 
The other two councilmen o~m property within one-half mlLe of 
this site as do 90% of the population of this City of 1,000 resi-
dents. 
Parcel 3 is owTIed by the C.R.A., and one-half of Parcel 4 
is ol;med by the owner of Parcel 5. 


Fair Political Practices 
Commission 
Mr. Leydeigh 
November 26, 1985 
-2-
The C.R.A. wishes to sell Parcel 3 to a developer. 
The developer 
wants Parcels 1 and 2 and perhaps Parcel 4 as well. 
I am not 
worried about FPPC because I will abstain on all votes and will 
not otherwise participate. 
After Call v Thompson, I am worried 
about 1090 questions. 
If the developer buys my Tand to make a 
better development, how can I get an answer to my worries about 
1090? 
Is there any way you can get an answer from the Attorney 
Ce~er&l? 
Very truly yours, 
PM/ap
